description_long: This object consists of a group of four hand grenades, each with distinct designs and markings. Two of the grenades are cylindrical with dark brown casings, labeled 'FLAME GRENADE' and featuring hazard symbols and metallic pull rings. Another grenade is spherical, olive green, and has a classic pin and lever mechanism. The fourth grenade is a larger, more complex cylinder with a black and cyan segmented body, labeled with both English and Asian characters, and features a prominent top cap and pull ring. The surfaces are mostly matte with some glossy highlights, and the grenades display various warning labels and hazard icons. The overall construction suggests a mix of metal and plastic materials, with visible seams, handles, and safety pins.
description: A group of four visually distinct hand grenades, including cylindrical flame grenades, a spherical green grenade, and a larger segmented explosive device with hazard labels.
description_view_0: Front view shows all four grenades, with the flame grenades and the large segmented explosive device most prominent, and clear hazard labels visible.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the cylindrical grenades and the spherical grenade in the foreground, with the pull rings and safety levers clearly visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the backs of the grenades, emphasizing the segmented structure of the large explosive device and the uniformity of the cylindrical grenades.
description_view_3: This side view focuses on the flame grenade and the large explosive device, with the spherical grenade partially obscured and the pull rings facing forward.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation is likely initiated by pulling the safety pin and releasing the handle or lever, which may trigger an internal fuse or ignition system.
